engagementShape: fixed-scope discovery sprint (2 weeks), with optional implementation phase
introContext: Pat reached out via the Arvaya site after reading our case study on RAG over operational telemetry. They asked specifically about how we'd approach evaluation for a self-service analytics product.
callObjective: Confirm whether the Q3 self-service yard-analytics launch is funded, and identify who owns the vendor evaluation alongside Pat.
hypothesis: Northwind is committed to shipping a self-service analytics product by Q3 but has under-invested in the data-orchestration layer that a RAG implementation depends on. Their VP AI Engineering hire (Feb 2026) signals an in-house build instinct, so the meeting is a real choose-build-or-buy moment.
meetingContext: This is a follow-up to Pat's inbound message. The AE has not yet seen Pat's roadmap — the meeting is intent-discovery first. A successful outcome is either (a) commit to a 2-week discovery sprint scoping the eval workflow, or (b) a clean 'we're building this in-house' that lets us walk away.
